scope.summary: Repair audit-wiring read fixtures by making the shared mock catalog prove both target-table cleanliness and dictionary visibility, so the tests assert audit wiring instead of failing at the semantic-read gate.
out_of_scope: Changing production dispatch, guard, audit, or database catalog-resolution logic.
Changing the moved module path introduced by the C6 split.

entry_points.name: wrong fixture assumption adjudicated
entry_points.trace: The old mocks returned empty rows for all ALL_POLICIES and ALL_TAB_COLS reads. Under the tightened semantic-read gate, empty visibility probes mean catalog-blind risk, not proof that the target table has no SELECT VPD policy or virtual columns. The repair keeps target-object probes empty where appropriate and returns a visible catalog row for the proof probes.

limitations: The proof uses an isolated repo-local target directory, target/nl105-probe, to avoid stale shared-target artifacts and custom-fingerprint churn; no live Oracle evidence is claimed.
The ordinary shared checkout target had stale test binaries during this session; those runs are not used as green evidence.
